segments.title: Short stream framing of ideal destroyers
segments.summary: The short stream frames ideal destroyers as period-specific benchmarks whose shield, spear, and general-purpose roles vary by national need.
segments.body: The discussion captures the usable substantive opening before the stream stops for audio troubleshooting. Dr. Clarke explains that the pictured ships are benchmarks rather than always the single best destroyer, then defines ideal destroyers through national requirements: some navies prioritize a defensive shield role, others a torpedo-oriented spear role, and general-purpose forces add anti-submarine and mine-laying capability alongside guns and torpedoes.

evidence.note: The opening says the pictures are benchmarks for discussing what an ideal destroyer would be for each period, not always the best destroyer of that period.
The role-framing section contrasts national destroyer needs: shield roles emphasize guns, spear roles emphasize torpedoes, and general-purpose designs add anti-submarine and mine-laying capabilities.
